title: HTML
subtitle: HyperText Markup Language
description: HTML is the standard markup language for creating web pages and web applications. It provides the structure of a webpage, allowing you to define elements such as headings, paragraphs, links, images, and more.

title: CSS
subtitle: Cascading Style Sheets
description: CSS is a stylesheet language used to describe the presentation of a document written in HTML or XML. It allows you to control the layout, colors, fonts, and overall visual appearance of a webpage.

title: JavaScript
subtitle: Programming Language
description: JavaScript is a versatile programming language that enables interactive and dynamic features on websites. It allows you to create responsive user interfaces, handle events, manipulate the DOM, and build complex web applications.

title: Node
subtitle: Runtime Environment
description: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. It allows you to run JavaScript code on the server-side.

title: React
subtitle: JavaScript Library
description: React is a popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It allows you to create reusable UI components and efficiently update and render them based on changes in data.

title: Angular
subtitle: JavaScript Framework
description: Angular is a TypeScript-based open-source web application framework. It provides a comprehensive solution for building single-page applications with a focus on maintainability and scalability.
